name: AI Quality, Evaluation & Release Governance

scenarios.title: Create a durable AI quality release packet
steps: Open the Sprint 10 AI Quality, Evaluation & Release Governance workspace.

Review assistant audit events, evidence quality, feedback, review examples, prompt library items, release gates, automation policies, shadow runs, observability incidents, agent governance packets, advanced program packets, and action queue evidence.
expected: A durable AssistantAiQualityReleasePacket row is created with source summary, evaluation suite, grounding quality, prompt/model/tool change risk, automation regression, observability watch, release gate, rollback drill, response plan, and leadership summary JSON.
An AssistantAuditEvent records that packet creation is evidence-only.
No prompt, model, tool permission, automation policy, release gate, observability incident, runtime flag, deployment, or production behavior is changed automatically.

scenarios.title: Queue AI release governance review
steps: Add an optional AI quality release review note to a draft Sprint 10 packet.
expected: The packet moves to RELEASE_REVIEW_QUEUED and stores actionQueueItemId.
An AssistantActionQueueItem is created for ai_quality_release_review with the packet URL and release evidence.
Review remains separate from rollout; the assistant does not publish prompts, switch models, grant tools, enable automation, close incidents, deploy releases, or change runtime flags automatically.

scenarios.title: Approve a reviewed Sprint 10 packet
steps: Review the packet leadership summary, eval cases, grounding quality, release blockers, automation regression, observability watch, and rollback drill.
expected: The packet status becomes APPROVED with approvedAt, approvedByUserId, and approvalNote.
An AssistantAuditEvent records approval and reiterates the no-silent-release guardrail.
Approval marks the release governance evidence packet as reviewed only; actual prompt/model/tool/policy/runtime changes require separate downstream approved release workflows.

guardrails: No prompt is published, edited, approved, archived, or promoted automatically.
No model is switched, upgraded, downgraded, or deployed automatically.
No tool permission or agent authority is granted, expanded, certified, or revoked automatically.
No automation policy is enabled, paused, disabled, or executed automatically.
No release gate is bypassed or flipped automatically.
No observability incident is closed, downgraded, or resolved automatically.
No runtime flag, deployment, production behavior, customer-facing assistant answer, or external communication is changed automatically.
No training data export or model training job is started automatically.
